'''Morgan Smithies''' (born 7 November 2000) is an [[English people|English]] professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lk}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[NRL]] and both {{rlnt|England}} and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=24 October 2019}}</ref>

He previously played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Smithies was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire, England]]. He attended [[The Brooksbank School]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Bower|first=Aaron|title=How cup heartbreak as a junior fuels Morgan Smithies' desire for Challenge Cup success|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/cup-pain-morgan-smithies-wigan-23490069|work=Manchester Evening News|date=25 March 2022|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref> Smithies played his junior rugby at Siddal ARLFC, signing for Wigan in 2015.

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
In 2019 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921709|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 42-0 Catalans Dragons|publisher=BBC|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>
He played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8–4 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport|date=27 November 2020|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Smithies played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=28 May 2022|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>
On 1 June 2022, Smithies was banned for four matches over two dangerous high tackles which occurred during Wigan's Challenge Cup final win.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/morgan-smithies-wigan-warriors-banned-24100672/|title=Morgan Smithies receives four-match ban despite avoiding punishment in Wigan Warriors' Challenge Cup win|work=Manchester Evening News}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, Smithies played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Bower|first=Aaron|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=[[The Guardian]]|date=14 October 2023|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>

===Canberra Raiders===
On 31 October 2023, it was confirmed that he would join [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] starting in the 2024 season on a three-year deal. In round 1 of the [[2024 NRL season]],Smithies made his club debut for Canberra in their victory over [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|title=NRL told they will 'love' Morgan Smithies following confirmation of Canberra Raiders move: 'He's all action'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nrl-told-they-will-love-morgan-smithies-following-confirmation-of-canberra-raiders-move-hes-all-action|date=31 October 2023|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>
Smithies played 24 games for Canberra in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-canberra-raiders-analysis-exclusive-20240911-p5k9ws.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: The 'wasted' enforcer Ricky Stuart must 'fire up' in 2025|website=www.nine.com.au|date=11 September 2024|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>
On 24 February 2025, it was revealed that Smithies had been briefly refused entry from the Canberra sides Las Vegas hotel after he became involved in a scuffle with teammate [[Hudson Young]]. Head coach Ricky Stuart later spoke to the media saying “I have spoken to the players who are very embarrassed,” Stuart said. “The behaviour was unacceptable. The club will deal with this strongly".<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-two-canberra-raiders-players-sanctioned-over-hotel-room-scuffle-las-vegas-season-opener-raiders-vs-warriors-round-1/news-story/c6765bbc78537056183f6a7f99566371/|title=Raiders reveal duo 'disciplined' over hotel scuffle ahead of Las Vegas season opener|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=25 February 2025|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>
Smithies played 25 matches for Canberra in the [[2025 NRL season]] as the club claimed the [[Minor Premiership]]. He played in both finals matches as Canberra went out in straight sets losing to both Brisbane and Cronulla.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2025-the-mole-exclusive-canberra-raiders-season-review-20250924-p5mxr2.html/|title=The Mole's season review: 'Immediate job' for Raiders as unfair 'chokers' tag threatens to cloud long summer|website=www.nine.com.au|date=24 September 2025|access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2019 he was selected for the [[England Knights]] against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

He made his full {{rlnt|England}} début on 29 April 2023 in the 64–0 victory over {{rlnt|France}} at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].
